items.vote_raw: 12345689-yea; 7-nay(Motion to approve staff’s recommendation with the following amendments to:1) Revise SDMC Section 98.1102, Definitions, by deleting the words “either of” from the first paragraph of the definition of “algorithmic device”; 2) Revise SDMC Section 98.1102, Definitions, by adding a subsection (c) to the definition of “algorithmic device” to read:“(c) A software or product used by a person conducting an appraisal that does not recommend rental rates during the runtime operation of the software or product”; and 3) Revise the language in SDMC Section 98.1104, Remedies, subsection (a) to read:“A tenant may seek injunctive relief, damages, or civil penalties of up to $1,000 per violation of this Division, in a civil action against a landlord. In an action brought under this Division, a prevailing landlord or tenant shall recover costs and reasonable attorney’s fees. A prevailing tenant includes a tenant granted an order for injunctive relief. A lease provision that limits a landlord or tenant from recovering attorney’s fees shall not be enforceable against a landlord’s or tenant’s claim for attorney’s fees that arise under this Division.”)Amendment Failed2357-yea; 14689-nay(Motion to approve staff’s recommendations with the following amendments to:1) Revise SDMC Section 98.1102, Definitions, subsection (a), by deleting the words “that is more than 90 days old”; and 2) Revise SDMC Section 98.1102, Definitions, subsection (c), by adding the words “or benchmarking” after the word “appraisal”.)

items.vote_raw: 2. Passed123567-yea; 489-nay(Motion to approve staff’s recommendation with direction to amend the 2025-2026 State and Federal Legislative Platform to:1) Replace language in State Section 5, Public Safety, number 30, to read “Support legislation that preserves the City of San Diego’s control over its deployment and regulation of automated license plate reader (ALPR) technology to ensure robust oversight, transparency, and privacy protections; as well as to prevent Federal intervention; and add language to the Federal section as a new number 26 to mirror State’s Section 5, Public Safety, number 30”; 2) Strikeout language on State Section Policy 7 under Focus 3.1, Innovation Economy; 3) Add Language to Focus 1, Homelessness, Housing, Affordability & Community Services, Federal Section by adding a new number 40 to read “Support legislation that attains funding for the Community Development Block Grant Disaster Recovery Program to rebuild disaster-impacted areas and provides crucial seed money to start the long-term recovery process”; 4) Add Language to Focus 2.1, Transportation Infrastructure, Mobility, & Vision Zero, State Section, by adding a new number 34 to read “Support legislation and funding that convert key underutilized highways and roads in the State into multi-modal corridors to reconnect communities divided by transportation infrastructure”; 5) Amend Language to Focus 3.2, Economic Development, Federal Section, number 5 to read “Support funding and measures to maximize resources and incentives available to benefit low and moderate-income communities, as well as Opportunity Zones, Promise Zones and the San Diego Promise Zone Alliance”; 6) Add Language to Focus 3.2, Economic Development, State Section, by adding a new number 23 to read “Support legislation that requires financial institutions to have a continuing and affirmative obligation to meet the financial services needs of the communities, including low-and moderate-income communities and communities of color, in which the covered financial institution conducts substantial business”; 7) Revise Language to Focus 5, State Section, number 32 to read “Support funding of programs and agencies that provide public safety, mental health or legal services for those charged with treatment-mandated felonies under the now Prop 36 statute”; and 8) Restore strikeout language on Focus 5, under Federal section number 5 to read “Support federal legislation and funding to address wildfire prevention, preparedness, and recovery, including home hardening, enhancing defensible space and brush management.”)
